title: Integration
summary: Free 9709 Pure Maths 3 integration lesson ‚ÄĒ reverse chain rule, integration by parts, and definite integrals with Cambridge mark-scheme habits.

sections.content: Paper 3 integration is harder than Paper 1: you meet **substitution**, **integration by parts**, and **partial fractions** setups. Marks are often split between choosing the correct method (M1) and executing algebra (A1).
‚ąę u dv/dx dx = uv ‚ąí ‚ąę v du/dx dx. Choose u to simplify when differentiated (LIATE: Log, Inverse trig, Algebraic, Trig, Exp).

sections.question: Find ‚ąę x e^x dx.
sections.solution:
Let u = x, dv/dx = e^x ‚Üí du/dx = 1, v = e^x.

‚ąę x e^x dx = x e^x ‚ąí ‚ąę e^x dx = x e^x ‚ąí e^x + C = e^x(x ‚ąí 1) + C.

sections.content: Evaluate definite integrals by finding the antiderivative, then **substitute limits**. For area between curves, ‚ąę (top ‚ąí bottom) dx over the intersection interval. Sketching prevents sign errors.

items: Always include + C for indefinite integrals
Check whether the integrand needs a substitution before parts
ECF can rescue later marks if your u/v choice is stated clearly
Show boundary substitution explicitly for A marks

sections.content: If integration by parts needs a second round, write each line neatly ‚ÄĒ examiners penalise missing brackets that make v du/dx ambiguous. After integrating, differentiate your answer quickly to sanity-check.
